title: F-16V Deliveries to Begin September 2026; US Reaffirms Arms Priority
detail: Taiwan's Defense Minister Wellington Koo confirmed on March 26 that deliveries of the delayed F-16V fighter jets are expected to begin in September 2026, following a delegation visit to Lockheed Martin's South Carolina assembly line. The US has established a special project team to accelerate delayed weapons programs. The US has an $8.28 billion backlog of arms yet to be delivered to Taiwan. A $14 billion arms package — the largest ever for Taiwan, consisting largely of PAC-3 and NASAMS air defense interceptors — remains ready for President Trump's approval and is expected to be announced after his now-postponed Beijing summit.

media.caption: F-16V deliveries to begin September 2026; US reaffirms arms priority for Taiwan

title: HIMARS Letter of Offer and Acceptance Deadline Expires
detail: March 26 marks the expiration deadline for Taiwan to sign the letters of offer and acceptance for 82 HIMARS multiple launch rocket systems, part of the $11 billion US arms package announced in December 2025. Taiwan's parliament unanimously authorized signing on March 13, and Taiwan's government was expected to complete signing before the deadline. Failure to sign would have risked Taiwan losing its place in the HIMARS production and delivery queue. The three other weapons packages — TOW anti-tank missiles, M109A7 howitzers, and Javelin missiles — had earlier deadlines of March 15.

title: Trump-Xi Beijing Summit Postponed 5–6 Weeks Due to Iran Conflict
detail: The planned Trump-Xi summit in Beijing (originally scheduled March 31 – April 2) has been postponed by 5–6 weeks, with Trump citing the Iran conflict as the reason. China confirmed agreement to Trump's request on March 18. The postponement delays a $14 billion arms package to Taiwan, which the White House had held back to avoid disrupting the summit atmosphere. Analysts warn that continued delays could embolden Beijing's position that Washington is using arms sales as diplomatic leverage, contrary to the US 'Six Assurances' commitments. A second $6 billion asymmetric defense package is also in the pipeline.

media.caption: Trump-Xi Beijing summit postponed 5-6 weeks due to Iran conflict
